title: Provider-neutral self-describing contract with optional guidance
description: Define how automation discovers and uses LEDGER without making any agent product or skill installation authoritative.
decision: Make the executable operation registry and versioned JSON schemas the complete public contract. Optionally embed version-matched guidance bundles for supported agent hosts, installed only by explicit command. Core workflows require no skill and public schemas contain no provider or transport vocabulary.
rationale: Tally must be provider-neutral while remaining easy for automation to use. Schema-first discovery prevents documentation bundles from drifting into a second authority.
alternatives: Rejected provider-specific public contracts, mandatory Codex or Claude skills, network-fetched instructions, and direct Hermes adapters inside LEDGER.
consequences: Guidance compatibility is testable but optional. Unsupported callers can rely solely on schema and stable operation semantics. Guidance cannot broaden capabilities or bypass validation.
status_notes: Replaces executable-owned skill dependency with a provider-neutral authoritative schema and optional versioned guidance.
